headline: JPMorganChase to Present at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference
summary: NEW YORK, April 27, 2026--Jamie Dimon, Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of JPMorganChase, will present at the Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference in New York City on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. (Eastern).

headline: Chime to Participate in J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 27, 2026--Chime® (Nasdaq: CHYM), America’s #1 Choice for Banking1, announced today that CEO and Co-founder Chris Britt will participate in a fireside chat at the J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference in Boston on Monday, May 18, 2026 at 10:25 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time (7:25 a.m. Pacific Daylight Time).

headline: JPMorgan Cuts Norwegian Cruise Line Price Target: Are Middle East Tensions Sinking the Cruise Trade?
summary: JPMorgan trimmed its price target on Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) stock to $18 from $19, keeping a Neutral rating. The cut reflects fieldwork showing North American consumer hesitancy to book Eastern Europe ocean cruise itineraries following the onset of the Middle East conflict. For long-term investors, the call signals a near-term booking headwind worth tracking ... JPMorgan Cuts Norwegian Cruise Line Price Target: Are Middle East Tensions Sinking the Cruise Trade?

headline: How The Halma (LSE:HLMA) Story Is Shifting With JPMorgan’s Higher Target And Neutral View
summary: Halma is back in focus after analysts lifted their core valuation markers, with one model fair value moving from £39.24 to £40.33 and JPMorgan raising its price target to 4,150 GBp from 4,000 GBp. That higher 4,150 GBp level, held alongside a Neutral rating, reflects a view that the shares can justify a slightly richer reference point without a clear shift in stance. headline: How The Investment Narrative For Privia Health Group (PRVA) Is Shifting After Mixed Analyst Views
summary: Privia Health Group’s fair value estimate has been nudged from US$31.75 to US$31.80 per share, a very slight price target adjustment that keeps the overall valuation view largely intact. This small move sits within a broader mix of bullish and cautious analyst commentary, with some firms lifting targets into the mid US$30s while others trim into the low US$30s.
